seoTitle: How to Get the Current Date and Time in n8n
metaDescription: Learn how to fetch and format the current date in n8n using expressions or the Date & Time node, including offsetting for future dates.
intro: Many n8n workflows need to know today's date, whether for logging, scheduling, or comparing timestamps, and there are a couple of ways to grab it. This guide covers using simple expressions for quick access and the dedicated Date & Time node when you need more formatting control.

steps.heading: Use an inline expression
steps.body: Inside any parameter field, use the expression editor to call the current date directly without adding an extra node.

steps.heading: Add the Date & Time node
steps.body: For more control, place the Date & Time node onto your canvas where you need a formatted timestamp.

steps.heading: Choose a format
steps.body: Set the operation to format the date and pick the layout and timezone that fits your workflow.

steps.heading: Offset the date if needed
steps.body: Use the offset option to calculate a past or future date, such as tomorrow or a week from now.

steps.heading: Verify the output
steps.body: Check the data preview to confirm the timestamp matches what you expect before connecting it downstream.

faq.question: Can I get the current date without adding an extra node?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use an inline expression inside any parameter field to reference the current date directly.

faq.question: How do I format the date differently in n8n?
faq.answer: The Date & Time node lets you choose a specific output format and timezone for your timestamp.

faq.question: Can n8n calculate a date in the future or past?
faq.answer: Yes, the Date & Time node supports offsetting by days or hours to calculate relative dates.

faq.question: Why would I use a standardized ISO timestamp?
faq.answer: ISO timestamps keep date formatting consistent when passing data to external APIs or databases.
